name: WiFiDuck (Spacehuhn)
description: SpacehuhnTech's WiFiDuck — a Wi-Fi-controlled BadUSB / keystroke-injection tool. This flashes the ESP8266 'Wi-Fi backpack' half (the web UI + serial CLI that stores and runs Ducky Script); on integrated boards (DSTIKE WiFi Duck, Malduino W) it drives a companion USB-HID microcontroller that types the injected keystrokes. *** Authorized testing on devices you own or are permitted to test ONLY — keystroke injection into a machine you don't control is unlawful in most jurisdictions. *** MIT. Cyber Controller flashes the pinned upstream release image and adds no capability of its own.
note: ESP8266 'Wi-Fi backpack' single merged .bin flashed at 0x0 via esptool --chip esp8266 (same path as esp8266_deauther). Release assets verified live against v1.1.0 on 2026-07-08 (gh api): the release ships dstike_wifi_duck_esp8266_*.bin + malduino_w_esp8266_*.bin (the esp8266 halves) alongside atmega32u4 .hex + atsamd21 .bin/.uf2 for the HID companions — asset_match takes .bin and excludes the atsamd21/atmega assets so only the two esp8266 board images are offered. Dual-MCU tool: this flashes the Wi-Fi/UI half only; the USB-HID MCU that performs the keystroke injection is flashed separately with its own .hex/.uf2 (out of scope for esptool). --flash_size detect so a board's exact size need not be pre-known. Real-hardware flash pending real-hardware verification per the Stage-5 gate.
